title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
core_claim: DECO is a sparse MoE Transformer architecture that uses ReLU-based routing, learnable expert-wise scaling, and NormSiLU to match dense Transformer performance under equal parameter and token budgets while activating only 20% of experts, with a claimed 3x real-hardware inference speedup over dense inference.
likely_red_flags: Claims are abstract-level only; experiment scale, model sizes, datasets, and hardware details are not provided in the candidate record.
Code and checkpoints are promised for future release, not available according to the abstract wording.
The 3x speedup claim depends on a specialized kernel and unspecified hardware, so generality is unclear.
Dense-comparable performance under identical budgets is a strong claim that needs careful baseline and training-token verification.
End-side deployment motivation is compelling, but storage, memory-access, and latency measurements are not detailed in the abstract.
